_**User**_

$progressPreference = 'silentlyContinue'
Write-Host "Installing WinGet PowerShell module from PSGallery..."
Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Force | Out-Null
Install-Module -Name Microsoft.WinGet.Client -Force -Repository PSGallery | Out-Null
Write-Host "Using Repair-WinGetPackageManager cmdlet to bootstrap WinGet..."
Repair-WinGetPackageManager -AllUsers
Write-Host "Done."

i need to install winget

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Providing Winget install instructions**</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

`winget` must be installed on **Windows**, not inside WSL/Linux.

Run this in an **elevated PowerShell (Run as Administrator)** on Windows:

```powershell
$progressPreference = 'SilentlyContinue'
Write-Host "Installing WinGet PowerShell module from PSGallery..."
Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Force | Out-Null
Install-Module -Name Microsoft.WinGet.Client -Force -Repository PSGallery | Out-Null

Write-Host "Using Repair-WinGetPackageManager cmdlet to bootstrap WinGet..."
Repair-WinGetPackageManager -AllUsers

Write-Host "Done."
winget --version
```

If `winget` is still not found afterward, install/update **App Installer** from Microsoft Store (that package provides `winget`), then open a new terminal and run:

```powershell
winget --version
```

If you want, I can also give you a one-liner to run this from WSL via `powershell.exe`.

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Providing non-admin WinGet repair steps**</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

You hit two separate issues:

1. **No admin rights** → your installs to `Program Files` failed.  
2. **Module/cmdlet not available** → `Repair-WinGetPackageManager` isn’t loaded because `Microsoft.WinGet.Client` did not install.

Use one of these paths.

## Fastest fix (recommended)
Open **PowerShell as Administrator** and run:

```powershell
$progressPreference = 'SilentlyContinue'
Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Force
Install-Module -Name Microsoft.WinGet.Client -Force -Repository PSGallery
Import-Module Microsoft.WinGet.Client -Force
Repair-WinGetPackageManager -AllUsers
winget --version
```

## No-admin fix (current user scope)
If you cannot use admin, run this in normal PowerShell:

```powershell
$progressPreference = 'SilentlyContinue'
Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Force -Scope CurrentUser
Install-Module -Name Microsoft.WinGet.Client -Force -Repository PSGallery -Scope CurrentUser
Import-Module Microsoft.WinGet.Client -Force
Get-Command -Module Microsoft.WinGet.Client *WinGet*
Repair-WinGetPackageManager
```

Then close/reopen PowerShell and test:

```powershell
winget --version
```

## If still not found
Install/update **App Installer** (this package provides `winget`) from Microsoft Store, then reopen terminal:
- Store product: `App Installer` (Microsoft)
- After install: `winget --version`

If you want, I can give you a **single copy-paste diagnostic block** that prints exactly what is missing (PATH, module, App Installer package).

---

_**User**_

i am trying to connect using alacritty , but i cannot copy the token when interactive coder login ask for it.

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Providing clipboard and login workarounds**</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

Use a non-interactive login so you don’t need to paste inside the prompt.

```bash
coder login https://<your-coder-url> --token "<your-token>"
```

If your token is in **Windows clipboard** (WSL + Alacritty), do:

```bash
coder login https://<your-coder-url> --token "$(powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Clipboard' | tr -d '\r')"
```

That bypasses the interactive “paste token” step entirely.

If you still want normal copy/paste in Alacritty, add in `~/.config/alacritty/alacritty.toml`:

```toml
[selection]
save_to_clipboard = true

[[keyboard.bindings]]
key = "C"
mods = "Control|Shift"
action = "Copy"

[[keyboard.bindings]]
key = "V"
mods = "Control|Shift"
action = "Paste"
```

Then restart Alacritty.  
If you use `tmux`, tell me and I’ll give the tmux clipboard fix too.
